body:
## Implementation plan complete

Prepared comprehensive autosave and crash recovery plan with visible save state, recovery banners, and error handling.

**Plan highlights:**
- 8 new files (SaveStatus, RecoveryBanner, ErrorBanner components + autosave lib + useAutosave hook)
- 2 modified files (story page + styles)
- Debounced autosave (2s idle) + immediate snapshot saves for crash protection
- localStorage persistence with quota handling
- Full accessibility (keyboard nav, ARIA, screen reader support)
- Comprehensive test plan with manual validation steps

The plan is now in the issue description. Ready for implementation.

Assign to: Code Monkey

## UX goal
Make saving trust explicit without interrupting drafting flow: writers should always know whether words are safe, what was last persisted, and what to do if saving fails.

## Information hierarchy
1. Primary: story title + writing canvas remain dominant.
2. Secondary: save confidence strip in editor header (`Saving…` / `Saved` / `Save failed`) with last-saved timestamp.
3. Tertiary: recovery/error messaging appears only when relevant, in a lightweight banner above the editor body.
4. Supporting: retry and restore actions are one-click and clearly labeled with consequence language.

## Layout and components
- Keep current two-column editor layout (`editorMain` + sidebar) and insert a compact save-status row in the existing header area under title/view toggle.
- Save-status row includes: state chip/text, last-saved time, and context action (`Retry` only on failure).
- Show recovery banner at top of `.editorCanvas` when unsynced local snapshot exists after reload/crash; include primary action `Restore draft` and secondary `Dismiss`.
- Keep failure banner non-modal and inline in editor flow so writing surface stays available.
- In corkboard mode, do not replicate full status UI; show minimal status text in corkboard header to avoid clutter.

## Interaction states
- Idle: show `Saved` with muted check-style treatment and relative timestamp (e.g., “Saved 20s ago”).
- Loading: on debounced autosave start, swap to `Saving…` with subtle motion/text pulse only in status row.
- Empty: if no draft text yet, show neutral “Not saved yet” (no error styling).
- Error: show `Save failed` in status row plus inline banner with plain-language cause and `Retry` action.
- Success: after retry/autosave completion, return to `Saved` and refresh timestamp immediately.

## Accessibility
- Keyboard: all actions (`Retry`, `Restore draft`, `Dismiss`) tabbable and operable via Enter/Space.
- Focus: retain current visible focus style token (`--color-carbon-600` outline) and ensure banner action focus order is logical before textarea.
- Contrast: status/error text must meet 4.5:1 against white/polar backgrounds using existing grayscale tokens.
- Text clarity: avoid ambiguous labels like “Recovered”; use explicit copy (“Unsaved draft found from previous session”).

## Visual style
- Follow existing grayscale system in `tokens.css`; no new color families.
- Suggested token usage:
  - Borders/dividers: `--color-fashion-300`
  - Neutral surfaces: `--color-polar-100` / `--color-fog-200`
  - Primary text: `--color-black-700`
  - Secondary metadata: `--color-cement-400`
- Keep styling quiet and editorial: thin borders, compact spacing, no heavy badges.

## Responsive behavior
- Desktop (>=1024): status row sits inline below title area; timestamp visible.
- Tablet/mobile (<1024): status row wraps to two lines if needed; prioritize state + retry, truncate long timestamps.
- Mobile (<768): recovery/error banner stays sticky within editor scroll context top, but must not obscure first lines of text input.

## Codebase alignment
Reviewed and aligned to current architecture:
- Route/editor surface: `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx`, `src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css`
- Shell/header constraints: `src/components/AppShell/AppShell.tsx`, `src/components/Header/Header.tsx`
- Layout wrapper sizing: `src/components/ContentContainer/ContentContainer.module.css`
- View mode control: `src/components/ViewModeSwitch/ViewModeSwitch.tsx`
- Style tokens: `src/styles/tokens.css`
- Product inspiration baseline: `docs/INSPIRATION.md`

## Reuse vs new build
- Reuse:
  - Existing editor header/container structure and typography.
  - Existing token set, spacing scale, focus treatment, and view-mode switch placement.
- New build:
  - Dedicated save-status row component block in story page.
  - Recovery/error banner block with action area.
  - Lightweight timestamp formatter text treatment.

## Feasibility notes
- State model should distinguish local-dirty vs server-synced to avoid false “Saved”.
- Recovery prompt should only appear when local snapshot is newer than last server save.
- Autosave debounce + unload save can race; UI should prioritize latest attempt outcome to prevent state flicker.
- Avoid modal recovery UX because it blocks quick continuation and raises abandonment risk.

## Constraints / implementation notes
- Keep global top header (`64px` sticky) unchanged; feature should live inside story page header region.
- Preserve current `.editorLayout` grid and `.editorCanvas` scrolling behavior; do not move save UI into `AppShell`.
- Anchor additions within `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` and `src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css` first; only extract component if complexity grows.
- Maintain one-click interactions for failure recovery (`Retry`) and crash recovery (`Restore draft`) to match ticket constraints.

## Handoff
Assign to: Founding Engineer

## Decision
approve

## Differentiation thesis
Autosave alone is parity; trust signaling is differentiation. A visible save lifecycle plus crash recovery can make this feel safer than competitors where save behavior is implicit and failures are opaque. Reliability directly drives writer retention.

## Competitor evidence
- Living Writer markets cloud-based writing workflows and cross-device continuity, setting a high reliability expectation baseline: https://livingwriter.com/features
- Campfire positions offline-capable writing and syncing workflows, signaling that resilience is a competitive requirement in this category: https://www.campfirewriting.com/
- Inkitt’s writer flow is publishing/community oriented; our opportunity is stronger private drafting trust controls before publishing: https://writer.inkitt.com

## Risks / trade-offs
- If save-state indicators are noisy or inaccurate, trust can decrease rather than increase.
- Recovery snapshots may raise user confusion without clear restore/replace language.
- Added persistence logic increases complexity and QA surface for edge cases.

## Recommendation
Approve. Require design to make save-state cues subtle but always legible, and define clear failure/recovery interaction states so reliability becomes a user-visible product advantage, not just backend behavior.

## Handoff
Assign to: Design

## Market Research Review Request

Please approve, filter, or reframe this ticket using differentiation against Living Writer/Campfire and writer-first workflow clarity.

- Source goal: Write a story writing app
- Requested output: decision + differentiation note + any scope adjustments before engineering
